tree.setup: You're calling a dental clinic to make an appointment. The receptionist, Xiao Bai, speaks at exactly the right volume and pace for phone calls -- she has perfected this over years. She has a cup of warm water on her desk that she sips between calls. Her calendar is color-coded by appointment type.
tree.setup_zh: 你给牙科诊所打电话预约。前台小白说话的音量和速度刚好适合电话——这是她多年练出来的。桌上有一杯温水，每个电话之间她喝一口。她的日历按预约类型用颜色分了类。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 您好，阳光牙科。请问有什么可以帮您？
turns.text_pinyin: nín hǎo, yángguāng yákē. qǐngwèn yǒu shénme kěyǐ bāng nín?
turns.text_en: Hello, Sunshine Dental. How can I help you?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her you want to make an appointment.

options.text_pinyin: nǐ hǎo, wǒ xiǎng yùyuē kàn yá.
options.text_en: Hello, I'd like to make a dental appointment.
options.feedback: Xiao Bai opens her color-coded calendar.「好的，您想约什么时候？」预约 is the formal word for 'appointment.' 看牙 literally means 'see teeth' -- perfectly natural.

options.text_pinyin: wǒ yá téng, xiǎng kàn yīshēng.
options.text_en: I have a toothache and want to see a doctor.
options.feedback: Xiao Bai's voice shifts to concerned efficiency.「疼得厉害吗？我帮您安排早一点的时间。」Mentioning pain often gets you an earlier slot.
Xiao Bai checks her calendar.「今天满了，明天下午可以吗？」有空 means 'to have free time' -- useful for scheduling.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个星期四下午两点可以吗？
turns.text_pinyin: zhè ge xīngqīsì xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn kěyǐ ma?
turns.text_en: How about this Thursday at 2 PM?

options.feedback: Xiao Bai writes it down in green ink (green means check-up in her system).「好的，记住了。请提前十分钟到。」Repeating the time confirms the appointment.

options.text_zh: 星期四不行，星期五可以吗？
options.text_pinyin: xīngqīsì bù xíng, xīngqīwǔ kěyǐ ma?
options.text_en: Thursday doesn't work. How about Friday?
options.feedback: Xiao Bai flips a page.「星期五上午十点可以。」不行 (bù xíng) is a direct but acceptable way to say something doesn't work.

options.text_pinyin: néng bu néng zǎo yìdiǎn? wǒ hěn téng.
options.text_en: Can it be earlier? I'm in a lot of pain.
options.feedback: Xiao Bai checks for cancellations.「明天上午有一个空的，您来吗？」能不能 is a polite 'can or can't' request form.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好的，预约好了。请带上医保卡。再见！
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo de, yùyuē hǎo le. qǐng dài shang yībǎokǎ. zàijiàn!
turns.text_en: OK, the appointment is set. Please bring your insurance card. Goodbye!

tree.cultural_note: Phone appointments in China are straightforward. Dental clinics (牙科, yákē) often ask you to arrive 10-15 minutes early for paperwork. 医保卡 (yībǎokǎ) is the national health insurance card.
